skill.name: coder
skill.description: Implement focused code changes, create files, run project checks, and summarize tradeoffs for development tasks.
skill.summary: A focused coding assistant for precise edits, refactoring, implementation, and verification.
skill.author: 0xSero

security_audit.summary: Static findings at SKILL.md:3 were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. No evidence found of cryptographic code, network reconnaissance, prompt injection, data exfiltration, or executable scripts. The broad tool access wording creates a minor operational concern, so publication is acceptable with low risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static finding points to SKILL.md:3, which is a natural-language description of code editing. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, encryption routine, or security-sensitive crypto usage appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains only marketplace-style wording about editing code. There is no code or reference to a weak cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static finding points to SKILL.md:3, which says the skill can use tools for coding tasks. No network scanning command, host discovery logic, external endpoint, or reconnaissance instruction appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line has no network target, scan verb, protocol, URL, or command. The phrase is broad capability text, not reconnaissance behavior.

content.user_title: Implement Code Changes with a Focused AI Coder
content.value_statement: Many coding tasks need careful edits that match an existing project. This skill directs Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to implement, verify, and summarize targeted changes.
actual_capabilities: Guides an assistant to write, edit, and refactor code.
Emphasizes existing code style and project conventions.
Supports creating or modifying files during implementation work.
Encourages targeted edits that satisfy explicit requirements.
Prompts verification with tests or checks when appropriate.
Requires summaries of changes, tradeoffs, risks, and follow-up work.
limitations: It provides instructions only and does not include executable helper scripts.
It depends on the host tool permissions granted by the user.
It does not define language-specific frameworks or testing commands.
It cannot guarantee correctness without suitable project tests or review.

use_cases.title: Implement a Small Feature
use_cases.description: Add a focused feature while preserving project style and expected behavior.

use_cases.title: Refactor Existing Code
use_cases.description: Improve structure or readability while keeping the change narrow and easy to review.

use_cases.title: Fix a Bug with Verification
use_cases.description: Apply a targeted fix and run relevant checks when the project supports them.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Simple Edit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a clear file change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the coder skill to update the requested file. Keep the edit minimal and summarize what changed.

prompt_templates.title: Implement a Feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the coder skill to implement this feature. Follow existing conventions, update related files, and run relevant checks.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the coder skill to refactor this area. Preserve behavior, limit unrelated changes, and explain any tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Complete a Verified Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request requiring diagnosis and validation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the coder skill to diagnose the issue, make the smallest practical fix, verify it, and report remaining risks.

output_examples.input: Add validation to the settings form and run the related checks.
output_examples.output: Updated the settings form validation, preserved existing component patterns, and ran the available form test suite.

output_examples.input: Refactor the data loader to reduce duplication.
output: Shared loading behavior was consolidated into one helper.
Call sites were updated without changing the public behavior.
Follow-up risk: broader integration tests would improve confidence.

best_practices: Provide the target files, expected behavior, and any constraints before asking for changes.
Ask for tests or checks that match the project and the risk of the edit.
Review the final summary for tradeoffs, risks, and follow-up work.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for broad rewrites when a small fix is enough.
Do not omit acceptance criteria for user-facing behavior.
Do not assume it can verify changes when the project has no runnable checks.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an AI assistant to make focused code edits, refactors, and implementation changes.

faq.question: Does it include scripts or automation?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill contains only instruction text and metadata.

faq.question: Can it run tests?
faq.answer: It tells the assistant to run tests or checks when appropriate and when tool permissions allow it.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no confirmed malicious behavior, with only low operational risk from broad tool wording.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when you need precise code changes that follow existing project style and include a clear summary.
